bullets.text: Iran has issued a warning that Israeli attacks in Lebanon could jeopardize the ceasefire with the United States, following Israeli Prime Minister's orders for strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs in response to Hezbollah's actions.

Ghana has enacted a new law criminalizing LGBTQ+ activities, causing widespread panic among community members who fear losing their homes, jobs, and access to healthcare if the legislation is ratified by President John Dramani Mahama.

The World Health Organization (WHO) is urging community cooperation to contain an Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo, following protests against protocols for handling victims' bodies in Ituri province.
Former environment minister Peter Garrett will lead an independent inquiry into the AUKUS defence pact, launched by a group of Labor veterans and public figures concerned about a lack of scrutiny.
Residents in Katherine have welcomed a $2 billion lawsuit over PFAS contamination, stating that it is time to hold large corporations accountable.
A national peak body reports that the hospitality sector is facing "unprecedented" cost increases for food and power, leading many businesses to close.

Anthropic, an AI company, has filed for an initial public offering (IPO), setting up a potential race with other major AI firms like OpenAI and SpaceX and testing investor interest in the booming AI sector.
Barry Diller's company People Inc. has launched an $18 billion takeover offer for MGM Resorts, causing the casino resort group's shares to jump by 15% following the bid.
Colombia's currency, bonds, and stocks rallied significantly after right-wing outsider Abelardo de La Espriella unexpectedly won the first round of the presidential election.
